dataset_title: Individualized fMRI-guided rTMS combined with Self-System Therapy versus mindfulness exercises in treatment-resistant depression
evidence_note: Grounding is thin: src e1 (landing_page) returned 0 chars (fetch failed/empty). All required_variables below are grounded exclusively in src e2 (repository_record, 1378 chars). No participants.tsv fields (age, sex, clinical severity/response scores) were shown in the evidence, so no project below assumes a clinical-outcome variable, a demographic covariate, or a subgroup/equity variable exists as a file/column -- only what e2's text explicitly states. This is why the 'equity' lens is not used: nothing in the evidence shows a demographic/subgroup variable in this dataset, and why no project predicts 'clinical response' -- no response/severity-score variable is shown in the evidence.

projects.question_en: In this 28-patient treatment-resistant depression cohort, does active individualized fMRI-guided rTMS produce a larger pre-to-post (session 1 to session 2) change in resting-state functional connectivity than sham rTMS?
projects.question_es: En esta cohorte de 28 pacientes con depresión resistente al tratamiento, ¿la rTMS activa individualizada guiada por fMRI produce un cambio pre-post (sesión 1 a sesión 2) mayor en la conectividad funcional en reposo que la rTMS simulada (sham)?
projects.compute: Single workstation (16+ GB RAM) or university cluster account; fMRIPrep run for ~28 subjects x 2 sessions is CPU-bound, roughly 1-2 days wall-clock on a shared cluster node, no GPU required.

required_variables.name: Resting-state fMRI, sessions 1 and 2 (pre- and post-treatment)
required_variables.note: Session structure confirmed by 'Sessions: 1, 2' in the same source.

required_variables.name: Treatment arm assignment (active vs. sham rTMS)
required_variables.evidence_quote: received six weeks of active or sham repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) combined with psychotherapy

projects.still_open_because: Dataset was created 2026-06-29 (per e2 'Created' field), less than a month before this evidence snapshot, so no secondary analyses of it exist yet in the published literature.

projects.question_en: Within the same TRD patients, how does baseline (session 1) structural connectivity derived from diffusion-weighted imaging relate to baseline resting-state functional connectivity, and does this structure-function coupling shift from session 1 to session 2 after six weeks of rTMS plus psychotherapy?
projects.question_es: Dentro de los mismos pacientes con depresión resistente al tratamiento, ¿cómo se relaciona la conectividad estructural basal (sesión 1) obtenida por imagen de difusión con la conectividad funcional en reposo basal, y cambia este acoplamiento estructura-función de la sesión 1 a la sesión 2 tras seis semanas de rTMS más psicoterapia?
projects.compute: Diffusion tractography (MRtrix3 or DSI Studio) is compute-heavy; recommend HPC/cluster access or a lab GPU workstation; roughly a week of queued jobs for 28 subjects x 2 sessions of whole-brain tractography.

required_variables.name: T1-weighted (and T2-weighted) anatomical MRI, for parcellation and DWI-to-anatomical registration
required_variables.evidence_quote:
T1-weighted anatomical MRI
T2-weighted anatomical MRI

projects.still_open_because: The README (e2) documents structural, diffusion, and functional MRI as separate offerings but describes no prior analysis combining them; this is a brand-new dataset (created 2026-06-29) with no downstream publications yet.

projects.question_en: Using a difference-in-differences design across the pre/post sessions and the active-vs-sham rTMS arms, does active rTMS cause a larger shift in task-evoked prefrontal/striatal activation during the goal-priming task's match vs. mismatch trials than sham rTMS?
projects.question_es: Usando un diseño de diferencias-en-diferencias entre las sesiones pre/post y los grupos de rTMS activa vs. simulada (sham), ¿la rTMS activa causa un cambio mayor en la activación prefrontal/estriatal evocada por la tarea durante los ensayos de coincidencia vs. no-coincidencia (match vs. mismatch) de la tarea de goal-priming, en comparación con la rTMS simulada?
projects.compute: Single workstation; task-fMRI GLM per subject/session is lightweight (minutes per run); the full pipeline (fMRIPrep + first/second-level GLM) is feasible on a laptop or shared department server.
skills: task-fMRI GLM (FSL FEAT, SPM, or nilearn)
experimental design reasoning for quasi-causal inference from RCT-like arms

required_variables.name: Task fMRI runs for the goal-priming task: Prevmatch, Promatch, Prevmismatch, Promismatch conditions, sessions 1 and 2
required_variables.evidence_quote: Tasks: Prevmatch, Promatch, Promismatch, rest, Prevmismatch ... Goal priming task

required_variables.name: Treatment arm assignment (active vs. sham rTMS)
required_variables.evidence_quote: received six weeks of active or sham repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) combined with psychotherapy

required_variables.name: T1-weighted anatomical MRI (for normalization to standard space)

projects.still_open_because: No publication yet exists using this dataset (created 2026-06-29); the goal-priming task's four match/mismatch conditions across two sessions provide the ingredients for a within-subject causal contrast that has not been run.

projects.question_en: Can an existing pretrained neuroimaging foundation model (e.g., a public self-supervised sMRI/fMRI checkpoint) be fine-tuned in a few-shot regime on this 28-patient cohort's session-1-vs-session-2 resting-state scans to detect a treatment-related signature, and how does it compare to a connectivity-feature baseline trained from scratch on the same small sample?
projects.question_es: ¿Puede ajustarse (fine-tuning) un modelo de fundación de neuroimagen preentrenado y disponible públicamente (p. ej. un checkpoint auto-supervisado de sMRI/fMRI) en régimen de pocos ejemplos (few-shot) sobre los escaneos en reposo de sesión-1-vs-sesión-2 de esta cohorte de 28 pacientes para detectar una firma relacionada con el tratamiento, y cómo se compara con una línea base de características de conectividad entrenada desde cero en la misma muestra pequeña?
projects.compute: Single consumer/lab GPU (e.g., one RTX-class GPU) is sufficient for fine-tuning only (not pretraining); a public pretrained checkpoint must be downloaded, not trained from scratch, to stay within one student's semester.
skills: transfer learning / fine-tuning pretrained vision or fMRI foundation models

required_variables.name: Resting-state fMRI, sessions 1 and 2 (used as the fine-tuning/probe target: session/treatment-state classification)
T1-weighted anatomical MRI (for co-registration/normalization into the pretrained model's expected input space)

projects.still_open_because: The dataset is too new (created 2026-06-29) for any foundation-model transfer study to have been attempted on it yet; N=28 is exactly the small-sample regime where a few-shot transfer-learning evaluation is a realistic, well-scoped student contribution rather than training a foundation model outright.

projects.question_en: If a resting-state functional-connectivity classifier is trained to distinguish active-rTMS from sham-rTMS patients using data from patients in one psychotherapy arm (Self-System Therapy), does it generalize to correctly classify patients from the other arm (mindfulness exercises), as a within-dataset proxy for domain/subgroup generalization?
projects.question_es: Si se entrena un clasificador de conectividad funcional en reposo para distinguir pacientes con rTMS activa de rTMS simulada usando datos de pacientes de un brazo de psicoterapia (Self-System Therapy), ¿generaliza para clasificar correctamente a pacientes del otro brazo (ejercicios de mindfulness), como aproximación intra-conjunto de datos a la generalización entre dominios/subgrupos?
projects.compute: Single workstation; connectivity feature extraction plus a simple classifier (logistic regression / SVM / random forest) is CPU-only and runs in hours, not days.
skills: machine learning classification with small samples
careful interpretation of small-N generalization results

required_variables.name: Treatment/psychotherapy arm assignment (active vs. sham rTMS; Self-System Therapy vs. mindfulness exercises)
required_variables.evidence_quote: Name: Individualized fMRI-guided rTMS combined with Self-System Therapy versus mindfulness exercises in treatment-resistant depression ... received six weeks of active or sham repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) combined with psychotherapy

projects.still_open_because: This is a within-dataset stress test only (the evidence does not show a second, external dataset the model could be transported to), explicitly framed as a proxy for the generalization lens rather than true external-domain validation -- a genuine cross-site test would require an additional public TRD neuroimaging dataset not evidenced here.
